skill.name: skill-marketplace-publisher
skill.description: Publish a Codex or Claude skill to Skillstore, SkillMap, or similar public skill marketplaces. Use when you need to audit a skill for public safety, build a public package, create a public GitHub repo, or submit a repo URL to marketplace intake endpoints.
skill.summary: End-to-end workflow for publishing AI skills to public marketplaces with safety auditing and submission automation.
skill.author: Sliense-ysd

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate security audit and marketplace publishing tool. Static analysis detected 126 patterns that are primarily false positives: (1) Pattern definitions in public_skill_audit.py designed to detect secrets were flagged as containing secrets, (2) Markdown backticks in documentation were misidentified as Ruby shell execution, (3) Public API endpoints for marketplace submission were flagged as hardcoded URLs. The skill performs local file auditing, makes outbound HTTP requests to public marketplace APIs, and uses shell commands for git operations - all appropriate for its intended purpose.

medium_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive - Ruby Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 73 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md and references/marketplaces.md. These are markdown code block delimiters showing bash command examples, not actual Ruby code execution. All flagged lines are in documentation files demonstrating workflow commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified file contents are markdown documentation with backtick-delimited code blocks, not Ruby source code. No executable Ruby files exist in the repository.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive - Pattern Definitions Detected as Threats
low_findings.description: The public_skill_audit.py script contains regex patterns to detect secrets (SSH keys, API tokens, AWS keys). Static analysis flagged these pattern definitions as actual security threats. The script is a security detector, not a source of vulnerabilities.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Code review confirms lines 47-60 define HIGH_PATTERNS and MEDIUM_PATTERNS arrays containing compiled regex objects for secret detection, not actual secret values.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive - Hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: Public API endpoints for Skillstore (skillstore.io/api/submit) and SkillMap (skillmaps.net/v1/feedback) were flagged as hardcoded URLs. These are documented public marketplace submission endpoints required for the skill's core functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to documented public API endpoints verified in references/marketplaces.md. No authentication credentials are embedded.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive - Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Multiple files were flagged for 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are false positives from pattern matching on documentation text and file extensions, not actual cryptographic implementations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic libraries imported or used. Flags triggered by text patterns in documentation, not actual crypto code.

content.user_title: Publish skills to AI marketplaces with safety audits
content.value_statement: AI skill developers need a reliable way to publish their creations to public marketplaces. This skill provides end-to-end publishing with automated safety auditing, packaging, and submission to Skillstore and SkillMap.
actual_capabilities: Audit skill files for secrets, local paths, and non-portable content before publishing
Package skills in public-safe mode removing private references
Validate skill metadata including SKILL.md frontmatter and file structure
Create and publish clean GitHub repositories for marketplace submission
Submit skills to Skillstore via automated API with submission tracking
Send listing requests to SkillMap via feedback endpoint
limitations: Requires Python 3 for running audit and submission scripts
Marketplace submission requires a public GitHub repository URL
SkillMap listing uses feedback channel, not guaranteed self-serve submission
Does not support CrowdHub or unverified marketplace endpoints

use_cases.title: Independent Developer Publishing First Skill
use_cases.description: A developer completes a Claude skill and wants to submit it to Skillstore for public discovery. The skill audits their code, packages it safely, creates a GitHub repo, and handles the submission workflow.

use_cases.title: Team Preparing Skills for Organization Portfolio
use_cases.description: A team maintains multiple internal skills and wants to publish selected ones to marketplaces. The workflow ensures consistent auditing, packaging, and submission across all skills.

use_cases.title: Skill Creator Submitting to Multiple Marketplaces
use_cases.description: A creator wants maximum visibility by listing on both Skillstore and SkillMap. The skill handles different submission formats and tracks submission status for each marketplace.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Skill Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my skill at /path/to/my-skill for public publishing safety. Check for secrets, local paths, and portability issues. Report any HIGH or MEDIUM findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to quickly check if your skill is safe to publish without full packaging.

prompt_templates.title: Full Publishing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish my skill at /path/to/my-skill to Skillstore. Target mode is public. Create a GitHub repo under my account sliense-ysd with repo name my-skill-public. Run the full workflow including audit, packaging, validation, repo creation, and submission.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want end-to-end publishing with all safety checks and automated submission.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Marketplace Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Submit my published skill at https://github.com/sliense-ysd/my-skill to both Skillstore and SkillMap. For Skillstore, include notes about the public audit date. For SkillMap, use skill name 'My Helper Skill' and email dev@example.com.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have an existing public repo and want to submit to multiple marketplaces.

prompt_templates.title: Package Building Only
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a public package from my skill at /path/to/my-skill. Run analyze first to identify issues, then build with mode public. Output to the default skill-packages directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to create a distributable package but handle repo creation and submission manually.

output_examples.input: Run audit on a skill with no issues
output_examples.output:
Public skill audit: /path/to/skill
Summary: HIGH=0 MEDIUM=0 INFO=0
No obvious public-publishing risks found.

Analyzed skill structure
Identified 2 items for removal (local paths)
Built public package to /skill-packages/my-skill-public
Package contains: SKILL.md, agents/, scripts/, LICENSE

best_practices: Always run the public safety audit before publishing to catch secrets and local path references early
Use packaged public mode when your skill depends on internal files or contains business logic that should not be exposed
Verify your SKILL.md has valid frontmatter with name and description before submission
anti_patterns: Do not publish source skills that contain real API keys, database credentials, or internal URLs
Do not skip the audit step even for simple skills - hardcoded paths are common oversights
Do not submit to CrowdHub or unverified marketplaces without confirming current submission URLs

faq.question: What does the public safety audit check for?
faq.answer: The audit scans for likely secret values (API keys, tokens, private keys), local absolute paths, references to secret hubs, and non-portable content that should not appear in public repositories.

faq.question: When should I use packaged public mode versus publishing source directly?
faq.answer: Use packaged mode when your skill contains internal references, depends on local-only files, or has business logic to abstract. Publish source directly when the skill is already self-contained with no private references.

faq.question: Which marketplaces does this skill support?
faq.answer: Verified support for Skillstore (self-serve API submission) and SkillMap (feedback-based listing request). CrowdHub is not verified for this workflow as of 2026-03-26.

faq.question: What happens if the audit finds HIGH severity issues?
faq.answer: The workflow stops and reports the issues. You must fix or remove the flagged content before publishing. Common HIGH findings include actual API keys, private keys, or production credentials in code.

faq.question: Do I need a separate GitHub repository for each skill?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. Skillstore accepts subdirectory URLs if your skill is part of a larger repository. However, a dedicated repository is cleaner and recommended for marketplace submissions.

faq.question: How do I track my submission status after publishing?
faq.answer: Skillstore returns a submission_id and status URL upon successful submission. Save these for tracking. SkillMap uses a feedback-based system so follow up via their contact channels if needed.
